title: skill: ori-migrate
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-migrate.md. Bootstrap an existing project (e.g. promptnotes-vcsdd which already has docs/domain/) into ori structure: move docs/domain/ → .ori/domain/, detect completed DDD phases (especially 9 workflows + 11 ui-fields), and offer batch scaffold of detected features. Critical for dogfooding readiness — without this we cannot dogfood promptnotes-vcsdd.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file present. Skill detects existing Phase 9 / 11 output and offers batch feature scaffold with user confirmation.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-migrate.md. Migrates existing docs/domain/ → .ori/domain/, heuristically detects all 11 DDD phases (esp. 9/11), offers batch feature scaffold with user confirmation, writes migrate-report.md. Force-closed: independent of ori-ep5 (CLI work)

title: skill: ori-review (phase 6)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-review.md. Phase 6 of /ori-flow: thin wrapper that spawns the ori-reviewer agent in fresh context (default capability: reasoning → claude-opus-4-7 / deepseek-v4-pro / o1 etc. per current_agent). Skill ITSELF runs in main session; it shells out to spawn the reviewer per the agent definition. Single pass — at most one round-trip patch back to earlier phase if review finds issues. 次のアクション → /ori-finalize.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file spawns ori-reviewer agent (per agents/ori-reviewer.md). Single-pass review enforced (no infinite loops).
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-review.md. Thin wrapper spawning ori-reviewer agent via 'ori feature run --phase review' (fresh-context, capability=reasoning). Enforces single-pass (max 1 round-trip), human-flags on Pass 2 findings. Force-closed: independent of v55.17

title: skill: ori-impl-green (phase 4)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-impl-green.md. Phase 4 of /ori-flow: implement source code under src/contexts/<bc>/ until tests are GREEN. Follow ddd-typescript.instructions (DDD layout, branded types, Smart Constructors, Result types, side effects only in infrastructure/). Mark subtask checklist items as done in beads issue description. 次のアクション → /ori-refactor.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file produces code matching ddd-typescript.instructions. Tests transition RED → GREEN.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-impl-green.md. Writes minimal implementation under src/contexts/<bc>/{domain,application,infrastructure}/ matching ddd-typescript.instructions, updates beads checklist (no sub-issues), drives RED→GREEN

title: skill: ori-test-red (phase 3)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-test-red.md. Phase 3 of /ori-flow: write failing tests under .ori/features/<feature>/tests/. Follow ddd-test.instructions (vitest, fast-check for VO smart constructors, describe('feature:<id>', ...), it cites spec.md section). Hard stop if tests pass on first run (suggests spec/implementation hole — flag to user). 次のアクション → /ori-impl-green.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file writes failing tests in tests/. Skill stops and reports if test passes immediately.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-test-red.md. Writes failing tests under tests/, follows ddd-test.instructions (vitest, fast-check, spec.md cite). Hard-stops + bd human flag on GREEN-on-first-run. Force-closed: skill file is independent of v55.14 (which is a sibling skill, not a real impl dependency)

title: skill: ori-derive (phase 1)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-derive.md. Phase 1 of /ori-flow: synthesize .ori/features/<feature>/spec.md from manifest.yaml derives_from + the referenced domain docs. Adopt feature-spec.instructions: required sections 概要 / 入出力 / 不変条件 / テスト観点 / 実装ノート, each with {#id}. Must check feature existence (call 'ori feature exists' / 'ori feature suggest' for typo recovery — do NOT auto-create scaffold; ask user). 次のアクション → /ori-plan.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file checks feature existence before deriving. Generated spec.md matches feature-spec.instructions.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-derive.md as /ori-flow phase 1. Checks feature existence via 'ori feature exists/suggest' before deriving, enforces feature-spec.instructions 5 required sections, no auto-scaffold

title: skill: ori-ddd-11b-ui-grouping
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-ddd-11b-ui-grouping.md. Phase 11b (UI Feature Grouping, ori ORIGINAL — not in upstream distill-ddd): AI proposes UI feature grouping based on ui-fields content. Final decision recorded in ui-fields/*.md frontmatter as 'coherence.depended_by:'. After phase, offer batch scaffold of UI features ('ori feature new ui-<id> --type ui'). This is the last DDD phase — 次のアクション → /ori-flow <first-feature> to begin implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file unique to ori (not from upstream). Output writes depended_by to ui-fields frontmatter. Skill offers batch UI feature scaffold.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-11b-ui-grouping.md as ori-original phase. Writes depended_by to screen-*.md frontmatter, offers batch UI feature scaffold, points to /ori-flow as last DDD phase

title: skill: ori-ddd-11a-ui-fields
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-ddd-11a-ui-fields.md. Phase 11a (UI Fields): extract input UI fields from Phase 10 types. ori specific: FILE SPLIT — write ui-fields/index.md (cross-cutting) and ui-fields/screen-N.md per screen. 次のアクション → /ori-ddd-11b-ui-grouping.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file enforces screen-N split. Output passes lint.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-11a-ui-fields.md with mandatory screen-N file split, VO→UI control mapping, and 次のアクション pointing to phase-11b

Phase 9 (Workflows): DMMF pipeline structure. ori specific: FILE SPLIT — write workflows/index.md (summary table + open questions) and workflows/<id>.md per workflow. After phase completion, offer batch scaffold of workflow features ('ori feature new <each-id> --type workflow' with user confirmation). 次のアクション → /ori-ddd-10-types.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file enforces file-split. Skill offers batch feature scaffold at end of dialogue. Output passes lint.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-9-workflows.md with mandatory file-split (workflows/index.md + <id>.md), DMMF pipeline schema, and batch scaffold offer at phase end

title: skill: ori-ddd-5-aggregates

acceptance_criteria: Skill file enforces H2=Aggregate + required H3 sections. Output passes lint with full structure on a test case.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-5-aggregates.md enforcing H2=Aggregate with required H3 trio (構成要素/ビジネス不変条件/公開操作) and 次のアクション to phase-6

title: skill: ori-ddd-1-discovery
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-ddd-1-discovery.md. Distill-ddd Phase 1 (Discovery): elicit Core Domain + business drivers and write .ori/domain/discovery.md. Adopt ori conventions: {#kebab-id} on all H2/H3, coherence: frontmatter. Skill must end with '次のアクション' section pointing to /ori-ddd-2-event-storming, optional skip paths to phase 5, and ori lint validation step.
acceptance_criteria: .apm/skills/ori-ddd-1-discovery.md exists with name/description frontmatter, role section, dialogue procedure, ori lint step, 次のアクション section. Generated .ori/domain/discovery.md passes ori lint.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-1-discovery.md with name/description frontmatter, role/dialogue/lint sections, and 次のアクション pointing to phase-2 + optional skip to phase-5

title: Implement ori arch export --adapter=<name> command
description: CLI subcommand that reads .ori/architecture.md and invokes the specified adapter package to emit a native linter config. Defines the adapter interface contract: input = architecture spec, output = native config file written into the project. Also implements ori arch check that runs the native linter through the adapter. Adapters are distributed as separate npm packages under @ori-ori/arch-adapter-*.

title: Design .ori/architecture.md schema for feature-sliced architecture
description: Define the frontmatter schema for .ori/architecture.md that declares layers, allowed cross-layer dependencies, and public entry points. Must support both backend feature-sliced layers (shared, domain) and frontend FSD layers (ui-entity, ui-feature, ui-widget, ui-page). This becomes the language-neutral SSoT consumed by adapter generators (eslint, dependency-cruiser, import-linter, generic regex fallback). Rationale: VCSDD/CoDD lacked an explicit architecture spec, leading to feature-internal layout drift and uncontrolled cross-feature spaghetti.
notes: Schema MUST support multi-root projects via roots: [{path, language, layer_set}] so a single .ori/architecture.md can describe e.g. Tauri ({path: src, language: ts} + {path: src-tauri, language: rust}). Cross-root contracts live in a designated published_language path (e.g. src-tauri/src/shared/contracts/ generates src/lib/shared/ipc/ via tauri-specta). Each root selects its own adapter (eslint for ts, arch-adapter-rust for rust). Even if v0.1 implementation supports only a single root, the schema must reserve this extension point so multi-root can land additively without breaking existing files.

title: update: add 次のアクション section to existing 5 skills
description: Existing skills (.apm/skills/ori-init.md, ori-flow.md, ori-sync.md, ori-propose.md, ori-review-proposals.md) were authored before the 'next-action guidance' design pattern was adopted. Each one needs a '## 次のアクション' section appended that points to the natural next skills. Also update ori-flow.md to include the feature-existence-check + typo-confirmation flow (NEVER auto-scaffold; always ask user).
acceptance_criteria: All 5 existing skills end with a '次のアクション' section. ori-flow.md has the typo confirmation flow.
close_reason: Added 次のアクション section to all 5 existing skills (ori-init, ori-flow, ori-sync, ori-propose, ori-review-proposals). Added feature-existence-check + typo confirmation flow to ori-flow.md step 1 + 注意

title: skill: ori-finalize (phase 7)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-finalize.md. Phase 7 of /ori-flow: settle the feature's state inward — clear self dirty marks, surface --force-generated proposals from .ori/proposals/, close beads phase issues, update status.yaml derived cache. CONTRAST with /ori-sync which fans out detected changes; /ori-finalize concludes one feature. 次のアクション → /ori-flow on the next feature, or /ori-review-proposals if proposals were generated.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file scope = one feature. Closes beads phase issues, clears status.yaml.dirty[], surfaces proposals.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-finalize.md. Single-feature scope. Clears status.yaml.dirty[], closes phase issues, surfaces proposals from .ori/proposals/. Contrasts with /ori-sync (fan-out)

title: skill: ori-refactor (phase 5)
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-refactor.md. Phase 5 of /ori-flow: tidy code without breaking tests. Remove duplication, clarify names, but DO NOT add features. Capability: fast (cheap model is OK). 次のアクション → /ori-review.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file scope-limited to refactor only (no new behaviour). Tests stay GREEN.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-refactor.md. Scope-limited to tidy (rename/dedupe/extract). No new behavior. capability=fast (cheap model). Tests must stay GREEN throughout

title: skill: ori-plan (phase 2)

NO plan.md file output — all task breakdown lives in beads issue descriptions. Sub-tasks go as '- [ ]' checklists inside the impl-green issue description. 次のアクション → /ori-test-red.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file outputs to beads (no file artifact). Downstream issues have populated descriptions after phase.

acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists with full structure. Output passes ori lint.

title: ori MVP v0.1
description: Bundle the v0.1 minimum-viable scope: @ori-ori/cli implementation, APM skill suite (Groups A-G), DDD code templates, and dogfooding readiness on promptnotes-vcsdd. Tier 0 is the smallest subset that lets dogfooding start.

title: Implement ori init with template copy and initial domain scaffold
description: currently packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts creates .ori/ directories and writes .ori/config.yaml, but the --template <name> flag only prints a warning. Need to: (1) implement template copy from @ori-ori/templates into the project root, (2) seed .ori/domain/aggregates.md and other entry-point md files so /ori-distill has anchors to update, (3) honor --force on existing .ori/ contents. README.md:57 shows the target UX: ori init --template tauri-ts
acceptance_criteria: ori init --template ddd-typescript-tauri copies template files into project root AND seeds .ori/domain/*.md scaffolds. ori init (no --template) still works for domain-docs-only setup. --force overwrites existing files with confirmation.

title: Implement @ori-ori/templates ddd-typescript: feature-sliced scaffold (supersedes wmv)
description: Replace the deprecated ddd-typescript-tauri stub directory with a minimal feature-sliced TypeScript template. Structure: src/lib/{shared/{types,events,contracts}, <feature>/{domain,application,infrastructure,presentation,tests}}, each feature exposing only index.ts as public API. Includes .ori/architecture.md reference, eslint.config.js extending the eslint adapter output, vitest config, and one worked DDD aggregate example. Acceptance: pnpm install + pnpm test passes, ori init --template ddd-typescript produces a runnable project. Also removes packages/templates/ddd-typescript-tauri/ stub. Supersedes ori-wmv.

title: Implement @ori-ori/arch-adapter-eslint
description: Generates eslint.config.ori.js using eslint-plugin-boundaries from .ori/architecture.md. Supports both backend layers (shared, domain) and FSD UI layers (ui-entity, ui-feature, ui-widget, ui-page). Enforces: (1) same-layer imports prohibited, (2) public entry via <feature>/index.ts only, (3) cross-feature communication only through shared/contracts (events) or domain public APIs. User extends generated config in their main eslint.config.js.

title: skill: ori-feature-status
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-feature-status.md. Summarize one feature or all features: read status.yaml + beads issue states + dirty marks, render a concise table or per-feature breakdown. Wraps 'ori feature list' / 'bd show ori-feature-<id>' / status.yaml read.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists. Output gives at-a-glance progress per feature including current phase, dirty status, last activity.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-feature-status.md. Two modes: overview table (all features) and detail block (single feature). Integrates status.yaml / beads / git / proposals. Suggests next-action heuristically. Read-only

title: skill: ori-bug
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-bug.md. Triage a human-found bug into the 4 cases documented in README.md (domain / impl / spec / cross-feature). Asks the 4 triage questions, classifies the bug, and recommends the matching recovery flow (e.g. 'edit domain/aggregates.md → ori sync' for case 1). Does NOT execute the fix — only routes the user.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file follows README triage flow. Skill outputs case number + recommended CLI commands without auto-running them.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-bug.md. Triages bug into 4 cases via 4 README questions. Routes user to appropriate flow (domain edit + ori sync / failing test + impl-green / sync --force + proposal / new workflow feature). Does NOT auto-execute fix

title: skill: ori-model
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-model.md. Conversational wrapper over 'ori model show' / 'ori model set'. AI asks user about phase, capability, or model override and translates to CLI commands. Useful when user wants to switch the agent or downgrade review to deep capability for cost.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists. Skill correctly translates user intent into ori model CLI invocations.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-model.md. Conversational wrapper translating natural language intent (e.g. 'review を deepseek に') to 'ori model set' CLI invocations. Always confirms cost/quality tradeoff before applying

title: skill: ori-doctor
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-doctor.md. Full health-check: walk .ori/, run ori lint on every doc, detect dirty marks left unsync'd, compare status.yaml against beads, surface schema violations and broken cross-references. Report-only (does not auto-fix).
acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists. Running it on a healthy project reports all green; on a project with intentional violations it lists each issue with line numbers.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-doctor.md. Report-only health check covering 7 dimensions (schema/hash/status-sync/cross-ref/proposals/orphans/beads). No auto-fix. Outputs prioritized diagnostic report with fix-pointer per issue

acceptance_criteria: Skill file complete. Output includes language-specific type files.

description: Create .apm/skills/ori-ddd-8-glossary.md. Phase 8 (Glossary): ubiquitous language with optional cross-BC differences. → .ori/domain/glossary.md. 次のアクション → /ori-ddd-9-workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file complete. Glossary structure clear.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-8-glossary.md with category H2 / term H3 structure, cross-context differences table, 次のアクション to phase-9

title: skill: ori-ddd-7-validation
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-ddd-7-validation.md. Phase 7 (Validation scenarios): use-case walkthroughs (Given/When/Then), single file (not split). → .ori/domain/validation.md. 次のアクション → /ori-ddd-8-glossary.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file complete. Output uses Given/When/Then format.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-ddd-7-validation.md with Given/When/Then scenario format, single file (no split), feedback loop to phase-5/6 if scenario cannot be expressed

title: Implement ddd-typescript-tauri: derivative of ddd-typescript + tauri-specta contracts + Rust adapter wiring
description: Tauri variant of the ddd-typescript template (ori-d01). Adds: (1) src-tauri/ Rust feature-sliced scaffold (features/<f>/{domain,application,infrastructure,commands}.rs with mod.rs as sole public API), (2) tauri-specta integration auto-generating src/lib/shared/ipc/ from Rust contracts in src-tauri/src/shared/contracts/, (3) .ori/architecture.md multi-root configuration (TS root + Rust root with cross-root contract path), (4) Rust adapter (ori-yuk) wired into pnpm tauri dev pre-build. Inherits all TS-side conventions from ori-d01. Acceptance: ori init --template ddd-typescript-tauri produces a project that pnpm install + pnpm tauri build succeeds and arch lint catches cross-feature violations on both sides.
acceptance_criteria: ori init --template ddd-typescript-tauri produces a project that pnpm install + pnpm tauri dev runs (or at least builds). Demonstrates DDD layer separation in the scaffold.
notes: Deferred to post-MVP v0.1. Per architecture discussion (2026-05-14), Tauri-specific scaffolding is out of scope. The plain @ori-ori/templates/ddd-typescript (ori-d01) becomes the canonical baseline. Revisit only if there's explicit dogfooding demand for Tauri.

title: Implement @ori-ori/templates: ddd-typescript template body
description: packages/templates/ddd-typescript-tauri/ currently only contains .gitkeep stubs. Need a plain ddd-typescript template (no Tauri) as the simpler baseline: tsconfig.json, package.json with vitest, src/ entry, tests/, and a minimal DDD aggregate example. This becomes the default scaffold for non-desktop projects.
acceptance_criteria: packages/templates/ddd-typescript/ contains a working scaffold that pnpm install + pnpm test passes against. Referenced by ori init --template ddd-typescript.

title: Documentation: per-language/framework starter guides (Start with Languages, Frameworks)
description: Create docs/start/ directory in the repo with a top-level index page 'Start with Languages, Frameworks' and per-stack pages. Initial pages: docs/start/index.md (matrix of supported stacks with status: available/planned/experimental), docs/start/typescript-web.md (uses ori-d01 template), docs/start/tauri-v2.md (uses ori-775 template). Each per-stack page documents: (1) prerequisites (Node/pnpm/Rust toolchain etc.), (2) ori init invocation with --template flag, (3) generated structure walkthrough showing feature-sliced layout, (4) first-feature walkthrough (ori-distill -> ori feature new -> ori workflow), (5) arch lint setup (ori arch export + adapter). README.md top adds a 'Getting started' section linking to docs/start/index.md. Future expansion: Python (FastAPI/Django), Rust (axum), Go, Kotlin (Spring), etc. — index page reserves their slots as 'planned'. Why: ori targets multiple language stacks; without per-stack entry points users have to read the whole README and guess which template fits.

title: Implement @ori-ori/arch-adapter-rust
description: Rust-side adapter for ori arch enforcement. Delegates to cargo-modules (or a thin custom checker over rustc --emit=dep-info) to verify .ori/architecture.md rules against the Rust module graph: feature isolation, mod.rs as sole public entry, cross-feature direct use prohibition. Required for Tauri projects (src-tauri/) and any Rust workspace. Generated config or test stub lands at src-tauri/tests/arch.rs (or workspace equivalent).

title: ori-distill phase 11b: auto-derive UI layers from ui-fields depended_by
description: When phase 11b completes (UI feature grouping), read .ori/domain/ui-fields/screen-*.md frontmatter (depended_by, depends_on) and emit or update the ui-layer section of .ori/architecture.md automatically. This closes the loop from DDD distill output to architecture lint enforcement: the UI dependency graph derived in phase 11b becomes the source of truth for the eslint boundaries config. Manual edits to the generated section should be preserved via a delimiter convention.

title: Extend ddd-typescript template with FSD UI layers
description: Add src/lib/ui/{entities,features,widgets,pages}/ to the ddd-typescript template with one example screen demonstrating the FSD dependency direction: pages -> widgets -> ui-features -> ui-entities -> shared, plus ui-features -> domain/<f>/index.ts only. Includes an end-to-end example where a UI feature triggers a domain use case via the public API and reacts to a domain event through shared/contracts. Verifies the eslint adapter catches same-layer UI imports and bypasses of public_entry.

title: Implement @ori-ori/arch-adapter-generic regex fallback
description: Language-neutral fallback adapter that parses import/use/from statements via per-language regex (ts/py/rs/go/java) and checks against .ori/architecture.md rules. Used when no native linter integration exists for the target language. Lower precision than AST-based tools but zero-dependency and works for any text-based language. Ships as the default safety net for new language support.

title: skill: ori-graph
description: Create .apm/skills/ori-graph.md. Render the coherence graph as a Mermaid diagram: nodes = domain sections + features, edges = derives_from / references with edge type styling, highlight dirty nodes. Outputs to stdout or .ori/graph.mmd. Useful for design review.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists. Output is valid Mermaid syntax; dirty nodes visually distinguished.
close_reason: Created .apm/skills/ori-graph.md. Renders coherence graph as Mermaid (domain/workflow/UI node types, derives_from/references/upstream edges, dirty/orphan highlighting). Options: --save / --focus / --depth / --no-ui / --only-dirty

title: Fix init.test.ts citty CommandMeta type mismatch
description: pnpm typecheck fails on packages/cli/src/commands/init.test.ts:6:30 with TS2339: Property 'name' does not exist on type 'Resolvable<CommandMeta>'. citty's CommandMeta is now wrapped in Resolvable<T> (a function or promise) so direct property access no longer compiles. Likely need to await/resolve before accessing .name in the test. Production build (tsup) is unaffected since tests are not bundled, but pnpm typecheck should pass cleanly before next release.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm typecheck passes for @ori-ori/cli with no errors. Test still validates the init command meta correctly.

value: ori adopts feature-sliced architecture (NOT hexagonal/layered/onion). Backend: src/lib/<feature>/{domain,application,infrastructure,presentation,tests} with index.ts as sole public API. Inter-feature direct import is completely prohibited; cross-feature communication only via shared/contracts/ (domain events through shared/events/event-bus). Why: VCSDD/CoDD pain was (c) cross-feature spaghetti + feature change amplification, not (a) layout drift or (b) layer dependency direction.

ori architecture enforcement = SSoT (.ori/architecture.md) + pluggable adapters that compile to native linters. Adapter packages: @ori-ori/arch-adapter-eslint (primary, uses eslint-plugin-boundaries), @ori-ori/arch-adapter-generic (regex fallback, any language), planned: dependency-cruiser, import-linter (Python), ArchUnit (JVM). Command surface: 'ori arch export --adapter=<name>' generates native config; user extends in their lint chain. ori does NOT reimplement linting.

Tauri projects work cleanly with ori's feature-sliced design: the TS<->Rust language boundary IS the cross-feature isolation boundary, enforced physically (not by lint). Structure: .ori/ + DDD docs are language-neutral; src/ uses TS FSD, src-tauri/src/ uses Rust feature-sliced (mod.rs as sole pub use), tauri-specta auto-generates TS clients from Rust contracts at src-tauri/src/shared/contracts/. Each root in .ori/architecture.md picks its own adapter (eslint for TS, arch-adapter-rust for Rust). ori-775 reopened as d01 derivative.

ori UI architecture uses FSD layers: pages -> widgets -> ui-features -> ui-entities -> shared (one-way only). Same-layer imports prohibited. UI features may import domain/<f>/index.ts (public API only). Cross-UI state lives in shared/store/ (Zustand slices), URL params, or React Query. Phase 11b depended_by frontmatter IS the layer declaration source — feeds .ori/architecture.md auto-update.

Templates: ship ONE feature-sliced ddd-typescript template (not many). Tauri variant deferred from MVP v0.1 (ori-775 deferred, ori-wmv superseded by ori-d01). Template's primary value is demonstrating feature-internal skeleton + index.ts public API + .ori/architecture.md reference, NOT tsconfig/package.json scaffold (those rot fast).
